title: Project Economics, Energy Design, and Pedestrian Safety Discussion
summary: Discussion quantifies the full project economics: ~$600,000 in road closure fees per 90-day period, ~$1M building permit for two buildings, $253M hard construction cost for the Comstock dorms ($360M across all three SU residence halls), employing 600-700 workers. Buildings will use National Grid power with high-efficiency heat pumps but will not be energy-independent. Council clarifies that prior fatal dump truck incidents near SU campus were attributed to a separate University Ave housing project, underscoring pedestrian safety concerns. SPD recommended two new four-way stops on Ostrom Avenue, which have been well-received by the community. Chair thanks Hayner Hoyt and reaffirms the city's commitment to following its own permitting rules fairly.

political_signal.description: Chair signals council support for development balanced with fiscal accountability: 'We are making sure that we do the right thing in terms of the city permitting processes, making sure that we are following the rules that we have set in place which is fair for everybody.'

processing_notes: $360 refers to '$360 million in construction value' for all three residence halls
$500 is per the fee schedule unit pricing, not a standalone amount
Fatal dump truck incident referenced was from a separate SU-area project on University Ave
Segment 4 of 4 from Public Works Committee Meeting, Tuesday March 17th 2026
